LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY ORDER FOR SALE OF DWELLING AND UNDEVELOPED REAL PROPERTY SHOULD NOT ISSUE IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. PETITIONERS DID NOT PROVIDE INFORMATION REGARDING THE EXISTENCE AND AMOUNT OF LIENS ON THE PROPERTY AS REQUIRED BY CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 704.760(C) OR ANY INFORMATION REGARDING THE FAIR MARKET VALUE OF THE PROPERTY. DECLARATION OF ANTHONY C. SOLDATO FILED BY PETITIONER TERRY AUSTIN, TRUSTEE, OF THE AUSTIN FAMILY TRUST LOUISE AUSTIN, TRUSTEE, OF THE AUSTIN FAMILY TRUST

LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT PATRICE PHILLIPS' MOTION TO SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT IS DENIED. RESPONDENT FAILED TO SHOW THAT THE JUDGMENT ENTERED AGAINST HER IN THIS CASE IS VOID. THERE IS NO SUGGESTION THAT SHE WAS NOT PROPERLY SERVED WITH THE PETITION TO CONFIRM THE ARBITRATION AWARD. THAT CONFIRMATION IS NOW A FINAL JUDGMENT. HAD SHE HAD ANY VIABLE GROUNDS TO CHALLENGE CONFIRMATION OF THE AWARD, SHE NEEDED TO ASSERT THEM IN OPPOSITION TO CONFIRMATION IN THIS COURT. IN ANY EVENT, THE EVIDENCE SUPPORTS THE CONCLUSION THAT SHE WAS SERVED WITH THE ARBITRATION DEMAND IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE APPLICABLE RULES, AND THIS COURT COULD NOT HAVE REVIEWED A MISTAKE OF LAW EVEN HAD RESPONDENT TIMELY RAISED IT. PETITIONERS REQUEST TO PRESENT ORAL TESTIMONY AT HEARING IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-14-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: DENISE LEADBETTER, ESQ., 415.713.8680, FOR PETITIONER TERRY AUSTIN, TRUSTEE, OF THE AUSTIN FAMILY TRUST.
R. STEPHEN GOLDSTEIN, ESQ., AND ALAN HERZFELD, ESQ., 415.673.5600, FOR RESPONDENT PATRICE PHILLIPS.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: RESPONDENT PATRICE PHILLIPS' MOTION TO SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT IS Denied. Respondent failed to show that the judgment entered against her in this case is void. There is no suggestion that she was not properly served with the Petition to confirm the arbitration award. That confirmation is now a final judgment. Had she had any viable grounds to challenge confirmation of the award, she needed to assert them in opposition to confirmation in this Court. In any event, the evidence supports the conclusion that she was served with the arbitration demand in accordance with the applicable rules, and this Court could not have reviewed a mistake of law even had Respondent timely raised it. Petitioners request to present oral testimony at hearing is denied. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

Order To Show Cause Why Order For Sale Of Dwelling And Undeveloped Real Property Should Not Issue
Off Calendar Oct-24-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Order To Show Cause Why Order For Sale Of Dwelling And Undeveloped Real Property Should Not Issue is Denied without prejudice. Petitioners did not provide information regarding the existence and amount of liens on the property as required by Code of Civil Procedure 704.760(c) or any information regarding the fair market value of the property. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Confirm Contractual Arb Award; Petition;
Off Calendar Jun-28-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Susan K. Breed, Esq., Tel. 415.572.5015, appearing for Petitioners and moving party.

No other appearance. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONERS TERRY AUSTIN, TRUSTEE OF THE AUSTIN FAMILY TRUST and LOUISE AUSTIN, TRUSTEE OF THE AUSTIN FAMILY TRUST's Motion To Confirm Contractual Arbitration award is Granted. No opposition filed. Order and Judgment signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.
